business_quality.rationale: Costco operates one of the most durable retail business models: a membership-anchored flywheel that generates high-frequency, low-margin merchandise sales supplemented by near-pure-profit membership fee income. FY2025 membership fee revenue grew 10% to $5.323 billion, paid members reached 81 million (up 6% YoY), and U.S./Canada renewal rates held at 92.3%. Q3 FY2026 extended this with 82.9 million paid members and a U.S./Canada renewal rate of 92.2%. Executive member penetration reached 41.2 million in Q3 FY2026 (up 9.6% YoY). The SKU-disciplined (<4,000 active SKUs per warehouse), depot-routed supply chain produces significant pricing authority. Kirkland Signature carries higher margins than national brands and drives loyalty. Ancillary businesses (pharmacy, gas, e-commerce) add traffic and loyalty at above-average growth rates. Digital comp sales grew 21.5% in Q3 FY2026, with AI-driven personalization delivering conversion 3x the site average. The business generates substantial operating cash flow ($13.335 billion in FY2025). Minor detractors: very thin retail margins mean cost-side surprises flow directly to operating income, and the business is highly concentrated in the U.S. and California.

risk_profile.rationale: Key risks include: (1) valuation at roughly $958/share embeds near-flawless execution; (2) membership growth deceleration to 4.1% paid members in Q3 FY2026, the lowest in several years; (3) tariff and macro exposure with management explicitly flagging monitoring of inflationary impact of higher oil prices and future tariff impacts; (4) geographic concentration with 86% of FY2025 net sales and operating income from U.S. and Canada, and California alone comprising 26% of U.S. net sales; (5) SG&A inflexibility from healthcare cost increases that offset core productivity gains in Q3 FY2026; (6) Section 301 litigation uncertainty around the member refund return process.

expectations_gap.rationale: At ~$958/share as of memo date, COST is priced for sustained high growth. Membership fee income growing 10% and comparable sales ex-gas/FX growing 6.6% in Q3 FY2026 are largely expected by consensus. The variant question is whether the market underappreciates: (a) the pharmacy/GLP-1 flywheel with significant market share gains; (b) AI search traffic inflection with triple-digit growth and the highest conversion rate of any traffic source; and (c) the durability of executive member upgrade momentum at 9.6% growth. Offsetting this, membership growth at 4.1% paid members is at or below consensus, and the stock already prices in long-run excellence, limiting expectations gap. Overall, expectations gap is narrow-to-moderate.

variant_perception.rationale: The primary variant is whether pharmacy/GLP-1 category expansion and AI-enabled commerce represent underappreciated growth vectors that, together with executive member upgrade momentum, can sustain comparable sales growth in the 6-8% ex-gas/FX range even as membership unit growth normalizes. A secondary variant is whether the Section 301 tariff refund creates a one-time loyalty and spend stimulus not yet priced in. Neither variant is strongly differentiated from visible consensus views, but the GLP-1/pharmacy and AI search opportunity are arguably undermodeled given their early stages and capital-light nature per management characterization.

valuation.rationale: With the stock at approximately $958/share as of memo date, COST trades at a material premium. FY2025 diluted EPS was $18.21 (up 10%); Q3 FY2026 EPS of $4.93 (up 15%) annualizes favorably but the implied multiple (~50x+ trailing) embeds a long-duration growth narrative that is fully visible to the market. Management noted that to achieve a similar yield to the prior special dividend, cash balances would need to be at a higher level than then, signaling no near-term catalyst from cash return at current prices. Valuation is the primary constraint on the overall stance.

catalyst.rationale: Near-term catalysts include: (1) Section 301 tariff refund processing and member credit expected to begin rolling in over the 2-3 months following the submissions period; (2) accelerated warehouse expansion toward 30+ net new openings annually in coming years; (3) continued AI/digital comp sales outperformance at 21.5% in Q3 FY2026; (4) pharmacy/GLP-1 market share expansion; (5) potential special dividend as cash balances grow. These are real but modestly visible; the stock likely already prices in the directional trend.

market_expectations: The market appears to price COST at approximately $958/share (as indicated by the Motley Fool transcript ticker display) as a near-perfect compounder: consistent high-single-digit comparable sales ex-gas/FX, 10%+ membership fee income growth, mid-teens EPS growth, and a durable membership moat with renewal rates above 92% in the U.S. and Canada. Consensus likely embeds approximately 6-8% comparable sales growth, continued executive member penetration gains, and gradual margin stabilization. The implied multiple (approximately 50x+ trailing earnings) demands sustained above-average revenue growth, minimal margin deterioration, and execution on the 30+ warehouse opening target without meaningful cannibalization. The consensus appears to treat membership growth normalization at 4.1% paid member growth in Q3 FY2026 as benign and does not appear to fully value upside from emerging pharmacy/GLP-1 and AI-sourced traffic vectors.

observations.statement: Membership fee income is a durable, high-margin revenue stream growing well above merchandise sales, driven by both fee increase carry-through and organic member base expansion; executive member penetration at 9.6% growth in Q3 FY2026 represents a higher-quality, higher-spending cohort.

supporting_evidence.quote: We reported membership fee income of $1.37 billion, an increase of $133 million or 10.7% year over year. Adjusting for FX, the increase was 9.9%. The September 2024 US and Canada membership fee increase accounted for a little more than 1/4 of membership income growth. Excluding the membership fee increase and FX, membership income grew 7% year over year.
At Q3 end, we had 41.2 million paid executive memberships, up 9.6% versus last year.

Membership fee revenue increased 10% in 2025, driven by new member sign-ups and membership fee increases. At the end of 2025, our member renewal rates were 92.3% in the U.S. and Canada and 89.8% worldwide.

observations.statement: Comparable sales growth of 6.6% ex-gas/FX in Q3 FY2026 reflects consistent member engagement, with management characterizing the 6-7% ex-gas comp range as stable and repeatable even as prior period tailwinds cycle off.

supporting_evidence.quote: Comparable sales were up 9.8%, and 6.6% adjusted for gas price inflation and FX. Excluding gas sales entirely, and adjusting for the impact of foreign exchange, comparable sales were also up 6.6%.

we continue to comp excluding gas in that. 6% to 7% range, and we have not really seen any variation from that performance as you look at membership spend and membership growth over the last year or so

observations.statement: Pharmacy is an emerging high-growth ancillary business driven by GLP-1 demand and expanded service offerings, generating significant market share gains with management viewing further expansion ahead.

supporting_evidence.quote: Pharmacy led the way and saw significant market share gains in the quarter. In addition to our experienced pharmacists taking great care of our members, a number of factors are contributing to this growth. These include increased GLP-1 demand, and inclusion of Wegovy and Ozempic in our Member Prescription Program, great value on pet medications, acceptance of Medicare D over-the-counter flex cards, and expansion of our mail order and specialty pharmacy offerings.

I think our buyers are right on top of the halo effect of GLPs and the needs of the members And I am quite impressed with what I am seeing from the CPGs, the rather big ones, and how they are pivoting to the future potential opportunities there. So there is quite a potential opportunity and, I feel we are on the front side of that.

observations.statement: AI-powered search and digital personalization are early-stage but showing triple-digit traffic growth and industry-leading conversion rates, representing a potentially underdiscounted incremental growth driver for e-commerce.

supporting_evidence.quote: While the volume of traffic generated from AI search is still low, we saw triple digit growth in Q3, and this activity had the highest conversion rate of all traffic coming to our site.
our personalized product recommendation carousels delivered conversion rates 3x better than our typical conversion rates. And contributed just under $5 billion of e commerce sales.

observations.statement: Paid membership growth decelerated to 4.1% in Q3 FY2026, attributed by management to the absence of large new-market openings rather than structural saturation; management views 4-5% as the normal rate without special events.

supporting_evidence.quote: we think the sort of the 4% to 5% is a more normal rate of growth when you do not have the benefit of a large increased that is linked to some kind of special event like COVID or a new market entry.
we ended the quarter with 82.9 million total paid members, up 4.1% versus last year, and a 149 million cardholders, up 4% year over year.

observations.statement: Gross margin management is disciplined but faces structural mix headwinds from higher-growth lower-margin segments; management explicitly invested in member value in Q3 FY2026 using a LIFO cycling benefit, resulting in a 9-basis-point core-on-core margin decline.

supporting_evidence.quote: Our reported rate was lower year over year by 21 basis points, coming in at 11.04% compared to 11.25% last year. Excluding gas inflation, the gross margin rate was higher by 1 basis point. Core was lower by 46 basis points and lower by 29 basis points excluding gas inflation. In terms of core margins on their own sales, our core-on-core margins were lower by 9 basis points.
Gross margin percentage increased 20 basis points. Excluding the impact of gasoline price deflation on net sales, gross margin percentage was 11.03%, an increase of 11 basis points.

observations.statement: Capital allocation is warehouse-expansion-first, targeting 30+ net new openings annually in coming years, with a growing cash balance and a management preference for special dividends when excess capital accumulates beyond reinvestment needs.

supporting_evidence.quote: In 2025, we spent $5,498 on capital expenditures, and it is our current intention to spend $6,000 to $6,500 during fiscal 2026.

we are in a position as you mentioned where we continue to generate excess cash beyond those priorities, and we believe that at our current valuation, special dividend is typically the most effective way to return excess cash without giving up the flexibility to keep investing in growth where we see opportunities to do that.

observations.statement: Tariff exposure and Section 301 refund claims create both near-term margin and litigation risk, but also a potential member loyalty stimulus if refunds materialize; management explicitly flagged anticipated further nonfood cost inflation from higher resin costs.

supporting_evidence.quote: We are closely monitoring the longer term inflationary impact of higher oil prices as well as the future impacts of tariffs.

How much we return and when depends on a variety of factors. Including how much refund money we receive, and when it arrives, as well as developments in the lawsuit filed against the company regarding the return process.
Higher tariffs are more likely to adversely impact rather than improve our results.

observations.statement: Costco's financial performance is highly concentrated in U.S. and Canadian operations, with California alone representing 26% of U.S. net sales, creating meaningful geographic concentration risk that constrains risk-adjusted return expectations.

supporting_evidence.quote: Our financial and operational performance is highly dependent on our U.S. and Canadian operations, which comprised 86% and 84% of net sales and operating income in 2025. Within the U.S., we are highly dependent on our California operations, which comprised 26% of U.S. net sales in 2025.

observations.statement: FY2025 operating cash flow of $13.335 billion and net income of $8.099 billion (up 10% YoY) demonstrate consistent cash generation; however, SG&A as a percentage of net sales increased 11 basis points in FY2025 and 20 basis points in Q3 FY2026 on a reported basis, primarily due to healthcare cost inflation and periodic one-time items, with core leverage achievable only at mid-single-digit comp growth levels.

supporting_evidence.quote: SG&A expenses as a percentage of net sales 9.25 % 9.14 % 9.08 %
Net income increased 10% to $8,099, or $18.21 per diluted share compared to $7,367, or $16.56 per diluted share in 2024.
outside of those, we would have seen a reasonable amount of leverage during quarter and more consistent with that idea of mid single digit comps delivering level of leverage.

variant_hypothesis: Consensus treats Costco as a steady 6-8% compounder with low variance, pricing in membership moat stability and gradual digital contribution. The variant is that three underappreciated growth vectors are in early innings and together can sustain or modestly exceed consensus comp and earnings growth expectations even as membership unit growth normalizes to 4-5%: (1) pharmacy/GLP-1 market share expansion including mail-order and specialty pharmacy; (2) AI-enabled e-commerce traffic conversion at structurally above-average conversion rates; and (3) accelerating executive member monetization in both existing and new international markets. A secondary variant is whether the Section 301 tariff refund, if executed, creates a one-time member loyalty and spend stimulus not yet in consensus numbers. The key variant perception is that pharmacy and AI search are capital-light and convex, meaning their incremental contribution could surprise to the upside without requiring large capital outlays.
why_consensus_may_be_wrong: Consensus models are likely extrapolating the recent 6-7% ex-gas comp range as stable but do not fully model: (1) the pharmacy/GLP-1 revenue acceleration, given that ancillary comp sales in Q3 FY2026 were up in the mid-20s led by pharmacy yet pharmacy remains a small and opaquely disclosed segment; (2) AI search as a new traffic acquisition channel with triple-digit growth and the highest conversion of any source, which is structurally different from paid search and could drive incremental high-income household acquisition as Costco's LLM relevance grows through product page optimization; (3) executive membership upgrade momentum with 9.6% growth and 41.2 million paid out of 82.9 million total paid, leaving substantial runway for upgrades from the Gold Star base, each of which structurally increases both fee income and merchandise sales penetration. Consensus may also underestimate the stickiness of gas station loyalty converting new first-time users, given management's comment that members who use gas stations spend more in the warehouse and renew at higher rates.

adversarial_research.bull_case: Pharmacy/GLP-1 and AI-sourced commerce are early-stage flywheels with high returns on incremental capital. Executive member upgrades have multi-year runway at minimal acquisition cost. Gas station loyalty is converting first-time visitors into higher-spending, higher-renewal members. The international pipeline in China, Spain, France, and UK provides long-term square-footage growth optionality. Warehouse expansion ramp toward 30+ annually adds operating leverage at scale. Tariff refund stimulus, if delivered, could drive a one-time member satisfaction boost and incremental spend. The business generates over $13 billion in annual operating cash flow with a pristine balance sheet and growing cash position.
adversarial_research.bear_case: The stock's approximately 50x+ trailing earnings multiple embeds virtually no execution risk and prices in decades of above-market growth. Membership growth at 4.1% is the slowest in years, and the normalization explanation may be self-serving given that digital sign-ups structurally renew at lower rates and are growing as a share of the membership base. SG&A deleverage from healthcare costs and legal settlements is recurring, not one-time. Tariff-related nonfood cost inflation from higher resin costs will pressure gross margins in coming quarters. The Section 301 lawsuit creates a liability and brand risk if the refund process is perceived as inadequate. Gas revenue inflation in Q3 FY2026 massively skewed headline optics. The warehouse count miss of two buildings in FY2026 signals real estate execution risk against the 30+ target.
adversarial_research.key_disagreement: The key disagreement is whether Costco's current comp trajectory of 6-7% ex-gas/FX can be sustained or accelerated without a major new market entry or fee increase, and whether pharmacy and AI-search growth vectors are material enough to offset structural headwinds from membership unit growth normalization and persistent cost inflation. Bulls believe these vectors are capital-light and convex; bears believe they are too small today to move consolidated metrics and that the multiple is simply too high for the actual earnings growth rate.

evidence_for_variant_view.quote: In digital, we saw strong member engagement in Q3, with site and app traffic up 37%. Pharmacy, gold and jewelry, home furnishings, tires, special events, housewares, and majors all grew double digits year over year.

These include increased GLP-1 demand, and inclusion of Wegovy and Ozempic in our Member Prescription Program, great value on pet medications, acceptance of Medicare D over-the-counter flex cards, and expansion of our mail order and specialty pharmacy offerings.
While the volume of traffic generated from AI search is still low, we saw triple digit growth in Q3, and this activity had the highest conversion rate of all traffic coming to our site.
This quarter, we launched our executive member program in China, and have seen strong early adoption in the market.

evidence_against_variant_view.quote: we ended the quarter with 82.9 million total paid members, up 4.1% versus last year, and a 149 million cardholders, up 4% year over year.
we currently expect to have 26 net new openings in fiscal year 20 down 2 buildings from the prior call with those 2 buildings now set to open in fiscal year 27.
we are anticipating further inflation in a number of nonfood categories as higher resin costs start to flow into cost of goods.
SG&A expenses as a percentage of net sales increased 11 basis points; three basis points excluding the impact of gasoline price deflation.

adversarial_research.rebuttal: The slowdown in paid member unit growth to 4.1% is a legitimate concern, but management provided a credible structural explanation in the absence of major new-market openings versus prior COVID and Asia expansion cycles, and the underlying growth composition is favorable: executive member growth at 9.6% and 7% organic fee income growth ex-fee-increase and FX are structurally more valuable than raw unit growth. The warehouse opening miss of two buildings is a one-quarter timing shift, not a capacity or execution failure, with management confirming those buildings open in FY2027. The nonfood cost inflation from resin is real but Costco has historically been the first to lower prices and last to raise them, which protects member trust and sustains traffic. AI search traffic, while low in absolute volume, is at an inflection: triple-digit growth from a small base with the highest conversion rate means even modest absolute scaling would be material to digital comps.
disconfirming_evidence: If paid member growth fails to re-accelerate even as new warehouses open in previously unserved markets, this would suggest the 4.1% slowdown reflects earlier maturation of the addressable membership base rather than a cyclical absence of new-market catalysts.

If pharmacy comp growth decelerates substantially as GLP-1 penetration normalizes or if pricing pressure from pharmacy benefit managers increases, the ancillary growth thesis loses its primary near-term driver.

If AI-sourced e-commerce traffic remains sub-1% of total traffic after multiple quarters despite triple-digit growth rates, the absolute contribution would be immaterial and the channel would fail to move consolidated comp sales in a measurable way.

If Section 301 refund litigation delays or significantly reduces the amount returned to members, the anticipated loyalty stimulus would not materialize and the brand risk of perceived non-delivery could modestly pressure renewal rates.

Any variant relying on management's forward characterization of pharmacy specialty and mail-order expansion is an unknown, as those revenue streams depend on carrier and PBM negotiations that are not publicly disclosed and cannot be independently verified from the attached documents.
adversarial_research.final_synthesis: Costco is an exceptional business with an entrenched membership moat, consistent cash generation, and multiple emerging growth vectors including pharmacy/GLP-1, AI-enabled commerce, executive member upgrades, and international expansion. The variant thesis that pharmacy and AI-enabled commerce are underdiscounted by consensus is directionally plausible but not strongly differentiated from visible bullish views on the stock. The primary constraint on a higher-conviction bullish stance is valuation: at approximately 50x+ trailing earnings, there is very limited margin of safety, and the stock prices in sustained execution over an extended period. Membership unit growth deceleration to 4.1% is manageable but warrants monitoring, and SG&A headwinds from healthcare costs are recurring. The research verdict is Candidate: the business quality warrants ownership consideration for long-duration investors, the variant perception around pharmacy and AI is worth tracking closely over the next two to three quarters, but the current valuation limits risk-reward without a meaningful pullback.

unknowns: The exact size and timing of the Section 301 tariff refund is contingent on legal proceedings and U.S. Customs and Border Protection processing speed, neither of which can be assessed from the attached documents.

The specific revenue and margin contribution of pharmacy including GLP-1, mail-order, and specialty pharmacy is not separately disclosed; ancillary comps at mid-20s are directional but insufficient to model the segment precisely. Any variant relying on management's forward pipeline for pharmacy specialty expansion is flagged here as an unknown.

The absolute revenue contribution of AI-sourced e-commerce traffic is described as still low volume despite triple-digit growth; the timeline to materiality and the sensitivity of conversion rates to scale are unknown.

The exact cadence and geography of the planned 30+ annual warehouse opening target beyond FY2026 is not specified; execution in China, Spain, France, and UK depends on regulatory and real estate factors not disclosed in sufficient detail.

The rate of mix change from digital member sign-ups, which renew at a slightly lower rate on average, and its long-term impact on the worldwide renewal rate trajectory are not quantified in either source document.
Healthcare cost inflation trajectory affecting SG&A: management cited this as a Q3 FY2026 headwind but did not provide forward guidance on healthcare costs as a percentage of SG&A.

The degree to which nonfood cost increases from higher resin costs will ultimately be absorbed by Costco versus passed through to members, and whether competitors will move in parallel, is unknown from the attached documents.

top_risks: Valuation risk: at approximately 50x+ trailing earnings, any deceleration in membership growth, comparable sales, or earnings growth could trigger a significant de-rating even if the underlying business remains healthy.

Membership growth structural deceleration: if paid member growth at 4.1% does not recover as new markets open, it may signal earlier-than-expected saturation of the domestic membership opportunity rather than a cyclical absence of catalysts.

SG&A cost inflation: healthcare costs and legal settlements are recurring and partially outside management's control; sustained headwinds could offset core productivity gains and compress operating margins.
Tariff and macro cost pass-through: anticipated nonfood inflation from higher resin costs could force margin trade-offs between member value and earnings protection, either of which carries risks.

Section 301 lawsuit: litigation around the tariff refund return process could delay or reduce the member credit, creating brand trust and renewal rate risk if members perceive the return as inadequate.
Warehouse opening execution risk: the FY2026 target was revised down by two buildings; sustained misses against the 30+ annual opening goal would pressure long-term revenue growth assumptions.

Geographic concentration: 86% of net sales and operating income from U.S./Canada with 26% of U.S. net sales from California means any California-specific economic shock would have an outsized impact on consolidated results.

bear.label: Bear
assumptions: Paid membership growth stagnates at 4% or below as domestic market matures and new-market international entries are delayed beyond FY2027.
Comparable sales ex-gas/FX decelerates to 4-5% due to slowing traffic growth and absence of incremental pricing power.
Healthcare costs and tariff pass-through pressures SG&A above 9.5% of net sales on an ex-gas basis over multiple quarters.
Section 301 refund litigation delays returns to members beyond one year, causing modest renewal rate pressure.
Multiple compresses toward 35x trailing earnings as growth rate concerns mount.
bear.implied_outcome: EPS growth decelerates to 6-8% annually; multiple contracts materially; COST underperforms the market over a two-to-three year horizon despite the business remaining fundamentally sound.

supporting_evidence.quote: we ended the quarter with 82.9 million total paid members, up 4.1% versus last year
SG&A expenses as a percentage of net sales increased 11 basis points; three basis points excluding the impact of gasoline price deflation.

base.label: Base
assumptions: Paid membership growth re-accelerates modestly to 5-6% as new warehouses open in international markets and executive upgrade momentum continues.
Comparable sales ex-gas/FX sustains at 6-7%, supported by pharmacy/GLP-1 gains, AI search conversion, and fresh and nonfood momentum.
Gross margin ex-gas is stable to modestly positive; SG&A leverages slightly at mid-single-digit comps with periodic healthcare cost offsets.
Warehouse expansion toward 30+ annual openings achieved by FY2027-2028.
EPS grows 10-12% annually; multiple sustains at approximately 45-50x as Costco's premium quality is reaffirmed.
base.implied_outcome: Stock roughly tracks earnings growth; investors receive a consistent compounder return with periodic special dividend optionality as cash builds. The stock produces market-like to modestly above-market returns without a meaningful re-rating.

supporting_evidence.quote: Net income for the third quarter came in at $2.19 billion or $4.93 per diluted share, up 15% from $1.9 billion or $4.28 per diluted share last year.
Net income increased 10% to $8,099, or $18.21 per diluted share compared to $7,367, or $16.56 per diluted share in 2024.

bull.label: Bull
assumptions: Pharmacy/GLP-1 and AI-search channels deliver outsized comp contribution, pushing ex-gas/FX comps to 8-9%.
Executive member penetration accelerates internationally including the China launch outperforming initial expectations per management, driving higher fee income and merchandise yield per member.
Section 301 tariff refund materializes and is returned to members, creating a one-time member satisfaction and loyalty event that boosts near-term renewal rates.
30+ warehouse openings achieved by FY2027 with strong build-back traffic in existing markets.
Multiple re-rates modestly higher as the market assigns credit to emerging pharmacy and AI-driven digital business value.
bull.implied_outcome: EPS growth accelerates to 14-16% annually; premium multiple is sustained or expands modestly; COST delivers above-market returns driven by the combination of operational execution and emerging growth vectors that surprise consensus to the upside.

supporting_evidence.quote: Digitally enabled comparable sales were up 21.5%, 20.8% adjusting for FX.
Overall, we have been very pleased. I think it is ahead of our expectations in China.
our personalized product recommendation carousels delivered conversion rates 3x better than our typical conversion rates. And contributed just under $5 billion of e commerce sales.

green_flags.trigger: Paid membership growth re-accelerates above 5.5% for two consecutive quarters, driven by new international market openings or executive member upgrade momentum.
green_flags.rationale: Membership unit growth is the most important leading indicator for future comp sales and fee income; sustained re-acceleration would validate the normalization thesis and reduce valuation risk.

evidence.quote: we think the sort of the 4% to 5% is a more normal rate of growth when you do not have the benefit of a large increased that is linked to some kind of special event like COVID or a new market entry.

green_flags.trigger: Pharmacy and ancillary comps sustain above 15% growth for two quarters, or management provides specific disclosure that pharmacy has become a top-three contributor to ancillary segment revenue.
green_flags.rationale: Pharmacy is the primary variant growth driver; sustained double-digit growth would provide confidence that GLP-1 and specialty pharmacy represent a durable share gain rather than a one-quarter event.

evidence.quote: Pharmacy led the way and saw significant market share gains in the quarter.

green_flags.trigger: Section 301 tariff refunds begin flowing to members without material legal delay, with management confirming the amounts and timing in a subsequent earnings call.
green_flags.rationale: Refund delivery would provide a one-time positive loyalty and spend event, validating management's member-first positioning and potentially surprising consensus expectations for near-term renewal rates.

evidence.quote: These submissions will go in over what may be the next few months and based on what other claimants have experienced, should start receiving refunds on approved claims on a rolling basis over the following 2 to 3 months.

yellow_flags.trigger: Comparable sales ex-gas/FX remain in the 5-6% range for two or more consecutive quarters without a compelling structural explanation, suggesting the 6-7% baseline may be gradually eroding.
yellow_flags.rationale: A sustained downshift in ex-gas/FX comp growth would signal that neither pharmacy nor digital is providing the incremental lift that the variant thesis requires, warranting a reassessment of the growth vectors.

evidence.quote: we continue to comp excluding gas in that. 6% to 7% range, and we have not really seen any variation from that performance

yellow_flags.trigger: SG&A as a percentage of net sales excluding gas inflation exceeds 9.25% for two consecutive quarters, suggesting healthcare or structural cost headwinds are outpacing productivity gains.
yellow_flags.rationale: SG&A leverage is a necessary condition for earnings growth at moderate comp levels; sustained deleverage would compress margins and challenge the bull case EPS trajectory.

evidence.quote: SG&A expenses as a percentage of net sales 9.25 % 9.14 % 9.08 %

yellow_flags.trigger: Warehouse net new openings in FY2026 or FY2027 come in below 24, meaning two or more consecutive years meaningfully below the 30+ target, suggesting real estate pipeline depth is lower than represented.
yellow_flags.rationale: Warehouse expansion is Costco's primary long-term revenue driver; consistent shortfalls would reduce the long-run growth trajectory and may indicate site scarcity or permitting challenges not yet disclosed.

evidence.quote: We opened 27 new warehouses, including three relocations, for a total of 24 net new warehouses: 15 in the U.S., two in our Canadian segment, and seven in our Other International segment

red_flags.trigger: U.S. and Canada membership renewal rate falls below 91% in any reported period, or management explicitly attributes the decline to a structural digital member mix effect that targeted marketing cannot offset.
red_flags.rationale: The renewal rate is the single most important metric underpinning the annuity-like fee income and member loyalty moat; a sustained decline would signal structural deterioration in the business model's most defensible characteristic.

evidence.quote: Our member renewal rate was 92.3% in the U.S. and Canada and 89.8% worldwide at the end of 2025.

red_flags.trigger: Paid membership growth decelerates to below 3% despite new warehouse openings in new international markets, or management acknowledges domestic saturation as a structural constraint rather than attributing deceleration to cyclical factors.
red_flags.rationale: Membership unit growth below 3% would signal that the total addressable market is effectively penetrated in core geographies, fundamentally challenging the long-run comp and fee income growth thesis.

evidence.quote: we ended the quarter with 82.9 million total paid members, up 4.1% versus last year

red_flags.trigger: Section 301 lawsuit results in a material adverse judgment requiring Costco to fund a large refund distribution that exceeds the amount recovered from U.S. Customs, or generates significant unexpected legal costs that impair near-term earnings.
red_flags.rationale: An adverse legal outcome could create a surprise cash outflow, distort near-term earnings, and generate negative member and press sentiment that pressure the stock at its premium multiple.

evidence.quote: developments in the lawsuit filed against the company regarding the return process

recommended_next_step: Add COST to a monitored watchlist at current valuation levels. Initiate a position only if the stock pulls back to a level implying a more reasonable earnings multiple (approximately 38-42x forward earnings), or if pharmacy and GLP-1 contribution is separately quantified in a future disclosure and proves materially above consensus estimates. In the interim: (1) track Q4 FY2026 and Q1 FY2027 membership growth for signs of re-acceleration above 5%; (2) monitor Section 301 refund processing timeline and any lawsuit developments in the next two quarters; (3) wait for potential analyst day or supplemental segment disclosure that sizes the pharmacy business more precisely to quantify the variant more rigorously; (4) track comparable sales ex-gas/FX on a monthly cadence for any sustained move below 6% that would undercut the base case thesis.
